request.question: Which company focuses on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health; Illinois Tool Works or Hologic?

title: Rain-X
Avery Berkel
ITW David Speer Academy
Shannon Free Zone
David Speer
W. James Farrell
Provena St. Mary's Hospital
Atossa Genetics
Hologic
Illinois Tool Works
sentences: Rain-X is a brand of consumer automotive and surface care products, including wiper blades owned by ITW Global Brands.
Until 2011 it was owned by Pennzoil-Quaker State Company dba SOPUS Products, a subsidiary of Royal Dutch Shell.
On March 1, 2011, Illinois Tool Works (ITW) has acquired SOPUS Products' Car Care business.
It became ITW Global, a division of Illinois Tool Works.

Avery Berkel, a major manufacturer of weighing systems and food processing equipment, was formed in 1993 when the British conglomerate General Electric Company plc combined their GEC Avery Ltd. (formerly W & T Avery Ltd.) business with the newly acquired Berkel company.
The group continued as a subsidiary of GEC (and later Marconi plc) until March 2000 when it was sold to Weigh-Tronix for £102.5 million to create Avery Weigh-Tronix.
In 2007, Avery Weigh-Tronix sold Avery Berkel to Illinois Tool Works.
ITW David Speer Academy (colloquially, Speer Academy) is a public four-year charter high school in the Belmont Cragin neighborhood in Chicago, Illinois.
It is a part of the Noble Network of Charter Schools.
It is named in memory of David B. Speer, the former Chairman of Illinois Tool Works (ITW), who served the company for 33 years until his death in 2012.
The school opened in 2014.
Shannon Free Zone is a 2.43 km2 , international business park adjacent to Shannon Airport, County Clare, Ireland which is 18 km from Ennis and 20 km from Limerick.
Businesses based on the site enjoyed special tax incentives on staff and profits until 2003.
This attracted a large number of multinational companies.
Currently there are over 100 international firms and 6,500 people employed at Shannon Free Zone in a diverse range of activities.

Companies who have invested at Shannon include Avocent, DeBeers Industrial Diamonds (Now Element Six), Kraus & Naimer, GE Capital, Precision Castparts Corp., Genworth, Ingersoll Rand, Intel, John Crane, Lufthansa Technik, Mentor Graphics, Molex, Illinois Tool Works, RSA Security, Ohshima, Schwarz Pharma and Zimmer.
David Blakeney Speer (April 6, 1951November 17, 2012) was an American businessman, best known as the CEO of Illinois Tool Works from 2005-2012.
W. James Farrell (born April 1942) is an American businessman, known for being the CEO of Illinois Tool Works from 1995 to 2005.
Provena St. Mary's Hospital is a non-profit hospital in Kankakee, Illinois.
It is a 182-bed acute care facility with a Level II Emergency/Trauma Center.

The hospital provides many health care services such as a Regional Cancer Center, Rehabilitation Services, Family Birthing Center, Pediatric Unit, Mental Health Services, Sleep Disorder Center, Breast Health Center, Renal Dialysis Centre, Accredited Chest Pain Center, Wound Care, Infusion Center, Breast Health Center, Occupational Health, Spirit of Women Program, and a Total Wellness Center.
It is part of the Presence Health healthcare system.
Provena St. Mary's is accredited by the Joint Commission and the Society of Chest Pain Centers (SCPC), as well as the American College of Surgeons Commission on Cancer.
Atossa Genetics NASDAQ: ATOS is a Seattle-based healthcare firm that specializes in breast health.
The company focuses on the development and marketing of pharmaceuticals for the treatment of breast conditions, including breast cancer.
Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products.
The Company’s core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health.

Its major products include the Selenia Dimension mammography system, the Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers, the NovaSure endometrial ablation device, the ThinPrep pap test, and the Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays.

While historically the Company was focused on women's health, expansion into the molecular diagnostics space has resulted in the Company's rebranding from "The Women's Health Company" to its current tagline, "The Science of Sure".
Illinois Tool Works Inc. or ITW is a Fortune 500 company that produces engineered fasteners and components, equipment and consumable systems, and specialty products.
It was founded in 1912 by Byron L. Smith.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

response.hop1_query: Which company focuses on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health; Illinois Tool Works or Hologic?
hop1_docs: Hologic | Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products.  The Company’s core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health.  Its major products include the Selenia Dimension mammography system, the Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers, the NovaSure endometrial ablation device, the ThinPrep pap test, and the Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays.  While historically the Company was focused on women's health, expansion into the molecular diagnostics space has resulted in the Company's rebranding from "The Women's Health Company" to its current tagline, "The Science of Sure".

International Journal of Women's Health | The International Journal of Women's Health is a peer-reviewed healthcare journal focusing on all aspects of women's health care, including obstetrics, gynecology, and breast cancer.  The editor-in-chief is Elie D. Al-Chaer (University of Arkansas for Medical Sciences).  The journal is published by Dove Medical Press and it is abstracted and indexed in EMBASE and Scopus.

Women's medicine in antiquity | Childbirth and obstetrics in Classical Antiquity (here meaning the ancient Greco-Roman world) were studied by the physicians of ancient Greece and Rome.  Their ideas and practices during this time endured in Western medicine for centuries and many themes are seen in modern women's health.  Gynecology and obstetrics were originally studied and taught mainly by midwives in the ancient world, but eventually scholarly physicians of both sexes became involved as well.  Obstetrics is traditionally defined as the surgical specialty dealing with the care of a woman and her offspring during pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium (recovery).  Gynecology involves the medical practices dealing with the health of women's reproductive organs (vagina, uterus, ovaries) and their breasts.

Patient Health Questionnaire | The Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ) is a multiple-choice self-report inventory copyrighted by Pfizer Inc, that is used as a screening and diagnostic tool for mental health disorders of depression, anxiety, alcohol, eating, and somatoform disorders.  It is the self-report version of the Primary Care Evaluation of Mental Disorders (PRIME-MD), a diagnostic tool developed in the mid-1990s by Pfizer Inc.  The length of the original assessment limited its feasibility; consequently, a shorter version, consisting of 11 multi-part questions - the Patient Health Questionnaire was developed and validated.

Epocrates | Epocrates, Inc. is an American company based in San Francisco, California that makes various mobile health software applications under the "epocrates" name, mostly for health care providers, providing them reference information about drugs and diseases, and diagnostic tools.

Diagnostic Health Corporation | Diagnostic Health Corporation, based in Birmingham, Alabama, is one of the nation's largest independent diagnostic imaging companies.  The company is the former diagnostic division of HealthSouth Corporation.  The company has network of 53 free standing diagnostic imaging centers located in 19 states and the District of Columbia.

Atossa Genetics | Atossa Genetics NASDAQ: ATOS is a Seattle-based healthcare firm that specializes in breast health.  The company focuses on the development and marketing of pharmaceuticals for the treatment of breast conditions, including breast cancer.
response.summary_1:
- Fact: [1] — "core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health." (Hologic page)
- Fact: [1] — Hologic is a "developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products." (paraphrase of opening)
- Missing/ambiguous entity: "Illinois Tool Works" — no passage in the provided set describes ITW; its product/focus areas are unknown from these passages.
- Bridge entities: Hologic (present, unambiguous); Illinois Tool Works (absent/ambiguous).
- Next-hop retrieval targets (prioritized):
  1) Fetch "Illinois Tool Works" (Wikipedia page or company overview). Extract infobox/business segments, "Products" or "Operations" section, and search for keywords: "diagnostics", "breast health", "gynecological", "skeletal", "medical imaging", "medical devices".
  2) (Confirmatory) Fetch "Hologic" (Wikipedia page or the cited Hologic page) and extract the same lines/section ("Business units" or lead paragraph) to verify the quoted focus and product list.
  3) If ITW Wikipedia page is inconclusive, fetch Illinois Tool Works' official website (About/Products) and any "Medical" or "Healthcare" business unit pages to confirm whether ITW focuses on the listed health areas.
- No final answer given here because Illinois Tool Works' focus is not present in the supplied passages; retrieve target (1) first to resolve the comparison.
response.hop2_query: Illinois Tool Works business segments products diagnostics medical devices
hop2_docs: Patterson Companies | Patterson Companies Inc is a medical supplies conglomerate primarily in the business of veterinary and dental products (ranging from xray equipment to consumable products, dental consumables make up the biggest part of the dental industry).  Since 2003 it also distributes general medical rehabilitative devices like braces and wheel chairs.  Traditionally a dental company it diversified its business at the turn of the millennium when it acquired 55-year-old company JA Webster Inc, a distributor of veterinary products.  That business segment (now known as Patterson Veterinary) currently distributes equipment (diagnostic and surgical) and medicine (anaesthetics, vaccines).  The company operates directly in only the US and Canada (in Canada through subsidiaries Sammons Preston Canada and Patterson Dental Canada).

PerkinElmer | PerkinElmer, Inc., is an American multinational corporation focused in the business areas of human and environmental health, including: environmental analysis, food and consumer product safety, medical imaging, drug discovery, diagnostics, biotechnology, industrial applications, and life science research.  PerkinElmer produces analytical instruments, genetic testing and diagnostic tools, medical imaging components, software, instruments, and consumables for multiple end markets.

Mindray Medical International Limited | Mindray Medical International Limited is China's largest medical equipment manufacturer, developing, manufacturing, and marketing a variety of medical devices, equipment and accessories for both human and veterinary use.  The company is currently organized into three key business lines: Patient Monitoring & Life Support, In-Vitro Diagnostic Products, and Medical Imaging Systems.

Hologic | Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products.  The Company’s core business units focus on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health.  Its major products include the Selenia Dimension mammography system, the Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers, the NovaSure endometrial ablation device, the ThinPrep pap test, and the Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays.  While historically the Company was focused on women's health, expansion into the molecular diagnostics space has resulted in the Company's rebranding from "The Women's Health Company" to its current tagline, "The Science of Sure".
Veredus Laboratories | Veredus Laboratories is a medical device company in Singapore, specialising in developing portable diagnostic tool kit.

Rain-X | Rain-X is a brand of consumer automotive and surface care products, including wiper blades owned by ITW Global Brands.  Until 2011 it was owned by Pennzoil-Quaker State Company dba SOPUS Products, a subsidiary of Royal Dutch Shell.  On March 1, 2011, Illinois Tool Works (ITW) has acquired SOPUS Products' Car Care business.  It became ITW Global, a division of Illinois Tool Works.

Eiken Chemical | Eiken Chemical is a Japanese company headquartered in Tokyo, established in 1939.  The company's business is manufacturing and sales of clinical diagnostics and equipment.  Major products of the company are fecal immunochemical test reagents and, immunological and serological reagents, microbiological reagents urinalysis test strips, clinical chemistry reagents, molecular genetics, medical devices and industrial products.
response.summary_2: Hologic focuses on diagnostics, breast health, gynecological surgical, and skeletal health. Hologic, Inc. is a developer, manufacturer and supplier of diagnostic products, medical imaging systems and surgical products, with major products cited including the Selenia Dimension mammography system, Discovery and Horizon bone densitometers, the NovaSure endometrial ablation device, the ThinPrep pap test, and the Aptima, Cervista, and Prodesse diagnostic assays.
